_meta.source: Comisión para el Esclarecimiento de la Verdad (CEV) — Informe Final 'Hay Futuro Si Hay Verdad' (2022)
_meta.primary_dataset: Proyecto conjunto JEP-CEV-HRDAG (Patrick Ball / HRDAG): integración + deduplicación + estimación estadística (captura-recaptura / multiple-systems estimation) sobre 112 bases de datos de 42 instituciones
_meta.note: Estimated figures correct for subregistro (under-counting), treated as a measurable object. Where the report prints two figures for the same item, both are noted.

statistics.label: Total victims of the armed conflict
statistics.notes: The Declaration frames non-combatant civilians as the overwhelming majority of victims.

statistics.label: Homicides / deaths in the conflict
statistics.responsibility_overlap: Categories OVERLAP and do not sum to 100% — 'guerrillas ~27%' already includes 'FARC-EP 21%' and 'ELN 4%'. Not a clean partition.
statistics.notes: Documented value 450,664 matches the bundled synthesis (HRDAG canonical integration figure); the report also prints 450,666 (l.8200) vs 450,664 (l.17051). Subregistro concentrated 1998-2004.

statistics.label: Forced disappearance
statistics.notes: Searchers (buscadoras) cite a running figure of 110,000+. Of CNMH's 80,670 registered, only 12.5% found.

statistics.label: Kidnapping / hostage-taking
statistics.notes: Guerrillas are the principal kidnappers; 78% men, 22% women.

statistics.label: Recruitment of minors (NNA)
statistics.notes: ≈44% were under 15 (a war crime under the Rome Statute). 11,314 boys / 4,924 girls. Ethnic minors over-represented.

statistics.label: Forced displacement (internal)
statistics.notes: The most generalized violation; ~1 in 6 Colombians; ~92% rural. The report's 'eight million'. Corte Constitucional T-025/2004 declared an estado de cosas inconstitucional.

statistics.label: Exile (forced departure abroad)
statistics.notes: RUV recognizes only 26,269 'victims abroad' (<2%). An invisibilized grave violation in itself. Top destinations: Ecuador, Venezuela, Spain, USA, Canada.

statistics.label: Selective assassinations (asesinatos selectivos)
statistics.notes: 'The elimination of those considered enemies was the most widespread way of making war in Colombia.' Peak 2002.

statistics.label: Massacres (≥4 killed, defenseless)

responsibility.paramilitaries: predominant, often with Fuerza Pública complicity

statistics.notes: ≈24,600 dead; affected ~62% of municipalities; peak year 2001 (405); most-affected dept Antioquia (29%).

statistics.label: 'Falsos positivos' (extrajudicial executions presented as combat kills)

responsibility.state_fuerza_publica: principal (Ejército Nacional), sometimes with paramilitary support

statistics.notes: A crime of State. All Army divisions implicated. The mothers' cry: '¿Quién dio la orden?'.

statistics.label: Sexual violence
statistics.estimated: massively under-reported (Casa de la Mujer: 82% never reported)
statistics.notes: ≈92% women and girls. Treated as a war crime and crime against humanity, not 'desire'.

statistics.label: Antipersonnel mine / UXO victims
statistics.notes: Used predominantly by guerrillas; the only indiscriminate attack with continuous increase. ~60% of victims were security forces.

statistics.label: Confinement (confinamiento)
statistics.notes: A modality of displacement (Corte Constitucional Auto 005/2009): communities lose mobility/autonomy over their territory. Concentrated in Pacífico/border zones; ethnic populations.

statistics.label: Land dispossessed / abandoned
statistics.notes: RUV registers only 31,863 despojo-violento victims (gross undercount). Colombia among 10 most land-unequal countries on earth.

statistics.label: Unión Patriótica political genocide
statistics.notes: Recognized as a political genocide; included a JEP macrocase (06).

statistics.label: Temporal concentration of victims
statistics.notes: Massacres peaked 1998-2002; falsos positivos peaked 2006-2008. The violence peaked as the war 'de-escalated', not at its start.

statistics.label: Violence after the 2016 Peace Accord
statistics.documented: 315+ FARC-EP ex-combatants killed (27 disappeared); 1,327+ social leaders/defenders killed
statistics.notes: Evidence the conflict persists ('esta guerra está viva'). Colombia #1 worldwide for HR-defender killings since 2016.
